editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Leslie Price requesting a weekly grant to Mrs. O'Leary.
Leslie Price (d. 1984) wife of Tom Barry, was involved in the 1916 rising and a prominent member of Cumann na mBan, later becoming director of the organisation. She also went on to become Chairman and President of the Irish Red Cross.

correspAction.placeName: Offices of the Irish National Aid and Volunteer Dependent's Fund, 10 Exchequer Street, Dublin, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Coimhlucht Conganta na nGaodhal
agus
Spleádhach Oglach na hÉireann
Longphort: 10 Sráid an Chiste, Áth Cliath Irish National Aid
and
Volunteer Dependents’ Fund
Offices: 10 EXcchequer St., Dublin 20th September 1916 Reply
27/9/16
Letter read
We are in touch
with Mrs O L + family
+ you may insure
all needs will
be attended to A chara
I wish you, if possible, to give some
weekly grant to Mrs O’Leary,
24, Steele’s Road, Haverstock Road,
London, N.W. I do not think
she has been on your lists.
Is mise
Le meas mór
Lesli Praidheas
Sec sub committee
Leslie Price?
